name: Valentine's Day
description: Cupid, candy hearts, red roses, and the saint who started it all.

questions.q: On what date is Valentine's Day celebrated?
What's the Greek name for Cupid, the god of love?
Who was Cupid's mother in Roman mythology?
Which Roman emperor was said to have ordered St. Valentine's execution?
Which Shakespeare play centers on the most famous Valentine's Day–style romance?
In what Italian city is 'Romeo and Juliet' set?
What flower is most traditionally given on Valentine's Day to symbolize love?
What hormone is famously called the 'love hormone' or 'cuddle hormone'?

source.notes: Released during hugging, kissing, and bonding.

questions.q: Which 1929 organized-crime massacre is forever associated with Valentine's Day?

source.notes: Feb 14, 1929 — 7 men killed in a Chicago garage. Linked to Al Capone.

questions.q: Which ancient Roman fertility festival held in mid-February is sometimes cited as Valentine's Day's pagan precursor?

source.notes: Held Feb 13-15. Banned in 5th century CE.

questions.q: Who founded Hallmark Cards and helped commercialize Valentine's Day cards in the U.S.?

source.notes: Hallmark started selling Valentines in 1913. Esther Howland is the 'Mother of the American Valentine.'

questions.q: What candies have personalized messages like 'BE MINE' and 'KISS ME'?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: Conversation candies date to 1866; heart-shaped Sweethearts since 1901. Necco produced them from 1990; Spangler took over the brand in 2018.

questions.q: Approximately how many heart-shaped boxes of chocolates are sold for Valentine's Day in the U.S. each year?
Which U.S. state produces the most roses for Valentine's Day?

source.notes: Most U.S.-grown Valentine's roses come from California, but ~80% of the rose supply on V-Day is imported (mostly Colombia, Ecuador).

questions.q: What 'X' at the end of a letter symbolizes today — going back to medieval times — was originally what?

source.notes: Medieval Christians kissed the cross they signed as a pledge.

questions.q: What two emojis dominate Valentine's Day texting?
On Galentine's Day (the day before Valentine's), what is celebrated?

source.notes: Coined by Leslie Knope on 'Parks and Recreation' in 2010.

questions.q: Which city's tomb is said to inspire the famous 'Juliet's Balcony' tourist attraction in Italy?

source.notes: Casa di Giulietta receives ~5,000 letters to Juliet each year.

questions.q: What aphrodisiac fruit was once called the 'love apple' in Europe?

source.notes: Originally feared as poisonous in Europe; later thought to be aphrodisiac.

questions.q: Aphrodite, goddess of love, was said to be born from what?
choices: Both A and B (depending on legend)

source.notes: Botticelli's 'Birth of Venus' (1485) depicts the clamshell version.
